paper_title: Geomorphic Surface Mapping and Offset Measurements

methods.description: This method involves using satellite images and digital elevation models to characterize and interpret alluvial fan surfaces and fault traces in the study area.

steps.description: Landsat satellite images, Google Earth images, and high-resolution digital elevation models (DEMs) with a resolution of <0.2 m/pix constructed for specific sites were used.
steps.searchable_summary: use satellite images and DEMs for geomorphic mapping

steps.description: Several field visits were conducted to verify our interpretations of mapping and to choose suitable sites for further study.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field visits to verify mapping interpretations

methods.description: This method uses photogrammetry to create high-resolution DEMs for illustrating subtle morphology and making accurate measurements.

steps.description: Photos were acquired with a digital camera carried on a DJI Phantom 4 Pro UAV.
steps.searchable_summary: capture photos using UAV for photogrammetry

steps.description: The DEMs were created using Agisoft Photoscan software.
Several ground controlling points (GCP) were evenly placed throughout the survey area and measured using differential GPS (dGPS) to scale and position the 3D models.
steps.searchable_summary: use GCPs and dGPS to scale and position 3D models

methods.description: This method involves extracting topographic profiles and using software to determine vertical and lateral offsets of geomorphic features.

steps.description: Topographic profiles across the fault trace were extracted from the DEMs.
Fit trend lines to both the hanging wall and footwall surfaces by least squares linear regressions.
steps.searchable_summary: perform linear regression on hanging and footwall surfaces

steps.description: Use the mean value of vertical distance between the hanging wall and the footwall to determine the absolute height of scarps and the standard error.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate scarp height and standard error

steps.description: Use LaDiCaoz_V2.1 Matlab software to obtain optimal left lateral offsets of displaced geomorphic features.

methods.description: This method uses in situ produced cosmogenic 10 Be exposure dating to determine the timing of alluvial fan abandonment.

steps.description: Sediments exposed on these fan surfaces are mainly comprised of quartz-rich angular to subangular clasts or gravels.
steps.searchable_summary: identify quartz-rich sediments for exposure dating

steps.description: Use in situ produced cosmogenic 10 Be exposure dating method to constrain the abandonment timing of alluvial fan surfaces.
steps.searchable_summary: apply 10 Be exposure dating to determine fan abandonment timing
